name: Citrus-Marinated Pork Chops
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut pork chops about 1/2-inch thick.
2. Cut orange into 6 wedges.
3. Cover chipotle chiles with water. Let stand until softened, about 1 hour. Drain and finely chop the chiles.
4. Place pork chops in a shallow glass or plastic dish.
5. In a blender container, combine chopped chiles, vegetable oil, lemon juice, orange peel, salt, garlic, and thawed orange juice. Blend at low speed until smooth.
6. Pour the blended mixture over the pork chops. Cover and refrigerate for at least 3 hours, stirring occasionally.
7. Set oven control to broil.
8. Remove pork chops from marinade and set aside.
9. Place chops on a rack in a broiler pan with tops about 3 to 5 inches from the heat.
10. Broil until light brown, about 10 minutes.
11. Turn chops and brush with remaining marinade. Continue broiling until done, about 5 more minutes.
12. Garnish with orange wedges and serve.
